name: Cheniere Energy
summary: Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **CHENIERE +2.4% TO $241.07 — A US GAS/LNG BID ON THE HENRY HUB HEAT-SURGE + THE STRUCTURAL LNG-SUBSTITUTION PREMIUM.** LNG $241.07 (+2.4% 1D, -10.3% MoM). The bid is partly the East-Coast-heatwave-driven Henry Hub surge (+22.7% MoM, a WEATHER story, markets C7) flowing into the US gas/LNG complex, partly the structural LNG-substitution premium (Ras Laffan ~17% force majeure, 3-5yr recovery, NOT deal-contingent; US terminals near-max because Hormuz/Ras Laffan took ~1/5 of global LNG offline). Do NOT 100%-crisis-code: the Henry Hub leg is weather, not war. The structural LNG-basis bid is the durable component; the crisis-equity component reverses on a deal / Lebanon cap. PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **CHENIERE +5.0% — A FAST-LEG CRISIS ASSET BOUNCED ON THE RE-ESCALATION, BUT CUT THE ATTRIBUTION (THAILAND). LNG $239.20 (+5.0% 1D, -11.2% MoM) outran the broader energy complex on TWO drivers: (1) ~70% crisis — the Hormuz 'complete closure' threat + the Iran-attack legacy on Qatar's Ras Laffan (~20% of Qatari LNG capacity sidelined for years) push European/Asian utilities toward US Gulf Coast supply; (2) ~30% NON-crisis — Thailand expanded its long-term Cheniere offtake from 1.0 to 1.3M tons/yr (commercial, independent of the crisis). DO NOT attribute the full +5% to the war — framing it as 'war re-pricing' is ~70% right. This is the LNG leg of the fast-physical premium re-entering (it had fallen -17% MoM in the May de-pricing). Reversible on a deal / Lebanon cap; the structural LNG-basis bid (Ras Laffan force majeure, 3-5yr recovery) is not deal-conditional. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30; Friday 29 May close). **CHENIERE -17% MoM — THE HORMUZ/LNG TAIL UNWINDS HARD ON DEAL-ANTICIPATION. LNG $224.86 (-2.1% 1D, -17.2% MoM, -9.5% 3M) as the market de-prices the Qatar-disruption / Hormuz-LNG tail premium that had bid the name through the crisis. This is the LNG leg of the same fast-physical de-pricing cracking fertilizer (urea -35% MoM, CF -11% MoM). Reversible on a deal collapse / Hormuz re-escalation; the slow legs (insurance, freight) have not unwound.** Largest US LNG exporter (Sabine Pass + Corpus Christi). **CHENIERE $232.03 (-0.9% 1D, -10.3% 1M, -1.3% 3M) — THE LNG-EQUITY CRISIS TRADE IS UNWINDING ON DEAL-ANTICIPATION + IRAN NON-RETALIATION, DESPITE TIGHT PHYSICAL (C6). The paper-physical bifurcation: Cheniere's equity sold -10.3% MoM as the crisis premium bleeds out of LNG names, but the PHYSICAL backdrop is unchanged — US LNG export terminals run at near-maximum utilization because the Hormuz/Ras Laffan disruption took ~1/5 of global LNG offline, and Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) force majeure is a 3-5yr recovery NOT contingent on the Iran deal. The May 25 US strikes went unanswered by Iran → the deal moved closer on paper → the LNG-equity crisis bid unwound. This is the SAME unwind hitting tankers (FRO -3.0%), fertilizer (CF -5.3% MoM), and defense (LMT -18.8% 3M) — the paper crisis trade is selling the deal while physical prices zero change. Reversibility: the ~25% crisis-linked equity component reverses on a deal break (Iranian rejection/retaliation OR a Beirut strike); the ~75% structural LNG-basis bid requires durable Hormuz physical reopening (Baker Hughes call: not until 2H26), not the deal text's freedom-of-navigation clause.** PRIOR (May 24): **CHENIERE $240.85 FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE (+0.2% 1D, -6.1% 1M, +8.1% 3M) — LNG-BASIS STRUCTURAL BID INTACT THROUGH SUNDAY (NO TRADE). NATURAL GAS HENRY HUB $3.02 (+15.6% MoM). TTF €48.69/MWh (+30%+ vs ~€31 baseline) — flat-to-easing but Qatar Ras Laffan FM 17% liquefaction persists as structural floor. TRUMP'S 'NO RUSH' SUNDAY PIVOT + 60-DAY MOU ARCHITECTURE DO NOT ALTER LNG-BASIS STORY — Qatar Ras Laffan FORCE MAJEURE IS STRUCTURAL-PHYSICAL, NOT DEAL-CONDITIONAL. STRUCTURAL-BID TRIO (XLE-FRONTLINE-CHENIERE-LOCKHEED) VALIDATED.** **Reversibility**: crisis-linked component (~25%) reverses on Pakistan-track failure (modest negative); structural LNG-basis (~75%) requires durable Hormuz physical reopening — not the leaked text's freedom-of-navigation clause but actual VLCC/LNG-tanker resumption. PRIOR (May 23): CHENIERE $240.85 MAY 22 CLOSE — LNG-BASIS STRUCTURAL BID INTACT. STRUCTURAL-BID TRIO (XLE-FRONTLINE-CHENIERE-LOCKHEED) VALIDATED.** Per markets dossier C8: Henry Hub $2.91 (-3.7% 1D EIA storage build, +11.2% MoM LNG-basis); TTF €48.69 (-1.46% 1D, +30%+ vs ~€31 baseline) — flat-to-easing but Qatar Ras Laffan FM 17% liquefaction persists as structural floor. Cheniere remains the cleanest non-Iran-conditional long inside the structural-bid trio. **Hormuz reopening (Baker Hughes call: not until 2H26) remains the only structural reverse.** Trump 'every day better' rhetoric and Munir COAS visit do NOT alter the LNG-basis story — Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure is structural-physical, not deal-conditional. **Reversibility**: Crisis-linked component (~25%) reverses on Pakistan-track failure (modest negative); structural LNG-basis (~75%) requires durable Hormuz physical reopening — not the leaked text's freedom-of-navigation clause but actual VLCC/LNG-tanker resumption. PRIOR (May 22): **CHENIERE $239.05 -0.6% 1D / -6.8% 1M / +7.3% 3M — LNG-BASIS STRUCTURAL BID INTACT EVEN ON DEAL-ANTICIPATION TAPE. Q1 DIVIDEND $0.555/SHARE PAID MAY 19; $1.2B DEPLOYED Q1 TOWARD GROWTH/REPURCHASES/DIVIDENDS PER 8-K. STRUCTURAL-BID TRIO (XLE-FRONTLINE-CHENIERE) VALIDATED FOR SECOND CONSECUTIVE CYCLE UPDATE.** **Structural thesis intact**: Q1 capital deployment $1.2B is consistent with sustained capital deployment off elevated EBITDA guide; Qatar Ras Laffan 17% liquefaction force majeure persists; EU storage refill window tightening into summer; ECB Lagarde 'new energy shock' framing carries forward. **The Hormuz reopening (Baker Hughes call: not until 2H26) remains the only structural reverse for this trade.** The paper-vs-physical bifurcation pattern present in Brent ($104.52, tail-risk de-priced) does NOT extend to LNG basis — TTF €47.69/MWh is a SHALLOW retracement (-2%) despite Brent -5.3% across window. **Cheniere is the cleanest non-Iran-conditional long inside the structural-bid trio (XLE + Frontline + Cheniere + Lockheed)** — same pattern as the May 19 brief flagged, now operationally validated for the second cycle update. **Wall Street structural thesis (multi-year US LNG export advantage; EU energy-security cycle)** is NOT vulnerable to Pakistan deal track per se — only to durable Hormuz physical reopening. PRIOR (May 19 — Tuesday): Created 2026-05-19 per markets dossier E1 — appears in 4+ independent sources as transmission mechanism between European TTF gas basis ↔ Hormuz LNG dislocation ↔ US LNG export economics. **Tuesday May 19**: +2.4% to $247.74 on raised 2026 EBITDA guide to $7.25-7.75B (Oilprice / 24/7 Wall St); EU storage 141 cargoes below 5-year low per Cheniere management commentary. **Structural thesis**: with Qatar LNG and Iranian gas off the global market via Hormuz blockade, European emergency-supply needs flow through US LNG exporters; Cheniere is the largest. The Hormuz reopening (Baker Hughes call: not until 2H26) is the only structural reverse for this trade. **Wall Street upgrades** post-EBITDA-guide raise: Cheniere is now positioned as the cleanest non-Iran-conditional long inside the structural-bid trio (XLE, Frontline, Cheniere). Recent moves UP on Brent-down day (+2.4% Tuesday despite Brent -1.6%) confirm war premium has migrated OUT of crude flat-price INTO LNG basis.

current.driver: ~25% crisis / ~mostly the US gas/LNG heat-surge bid: the East-Coast-heatwave Henry Hub surge (+22.7% MoM, WEATHER, markets C7) flowed into the US gas/LNG complex; plus the structural LNG-substitution premium (Ras Laffan ~17% force majeure, 3-5yr recovery). Do NOT 100%-crisis-code — the Henry Hub leg is weather, not war. Structural LNG-basis bid is durable; crisis-equity component reverses on a deal / Lebanon cap.

Physical backdrop UNCHANGED: Qatar Ras Laffan FM is a 3-5yr recovery, NOT deal-contingent. Reversibility: ~25% crisis-equity component reverses on a deal break; ~75% structural LNG-basis requires durable Hormuz reopening (Baker Hughes 2H26).

active_triggers.condition: Cheniere announces additional EBITDA guide raise within 60 days (by 2026-07-18)
active_triggers.note: Tuesday's $7.25-7.75B guide raise was off pre-war expectations; if European storage builds slower than expected through summer, another raise within 60 days would confirm structural-bid thesis.

active_triggers.condition: Hormuz reopens (Baker Hughes 2H26 timeline) — bearish reversal trigger
active_triggers.note: The only structural reverse for the Cheniere long thesis. Baker Hughes call: not until 2H26. Tracker: vessel transit returning toward ~138/day pre-war benchmark.

recent_signals.headline: **CHENIERE +2.4% TO $241.07 ON THE HENRY HUB HEAT-SURGE LNG BID + STRUCTURAL SUBSTITUTION PREMIUM.** LNG +2.4% as the East-Coast-heatwave Henry Hub surge (+22.7% MoM, WEATHER not war, markets C7) flowed into the US gas/LNG complex, layered on the structural LNG-substitution premium (Ras Laffan ~17% force majeure, 3-5yr recovery; US terminals near-max). Do NOT 100%-crisis-code — the Henry Hub leg is weather. The structural LNG-basis bid is the durable component; the crisis-equity component reverses on a deal / Lebanon cap.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-06-04 Section A (Cheniere $241.07 +2.4%), C7 (Henry Hub +22.7% MoM weather partly explains Cheniere)

Reversible on a deal collapse / Hormuz re-escalation; the slow legs have not unwound.
recent_signals.verification: Cheniere -17.2% MoM = CONFIRMED (data); driver (Hormuz/LNG tail de-pricing) = analyst assessment.

recent_signals.headline: **CHENIERE $232.03 (-0.9% 1D, -10.3% 1M, -1.3% 3M) — THE LNG-EQUITY CRISIS TRADE IS UNWINDING DESPITE TIGHT PHYSICAL (C6).** (1) **The unwind**: Cheniere's equity sold -10.3% MoM as the crisis premium bleeds out of LNG names on deal-anticipation + Iran non-retaliation (the US strikes May 25 went unanswered, intel A2). This is the SAME paper crisis-trade unwind hitting tankers (Frontline -3.0%, BOAT -2.0%), fertilizer (CF -5.3% MoM, Nutrien -4.4% MoM), and defense (Lockheed -18.8% 3M) — paper equities selling the deal. (2) **The bifurcation**: the PHYSICAL backdrop is unchanged — US LNG export terminals run at near-maximum utilization because the Hormuz/Ras Laffan disruption took ~1/5 of global LNG flows offline; Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) force majeure is a 3-5yr recovery NOT contingent on the Iran deal. Paper prices the deal; physical prices zero operational change. (3) **Reversibility**: the ~25% crisis-linked equity component reverses on a deal BREAK (an Iranian rejection/retaliation OR a Beirut strike, intel A3); the ~75% structural LNG-basis bid requires durable Hormuz physical REOPENING (Baker Hughes call: not until 2H26), NOT the deal text's freedom-of-navigation clause. Cheniere remains the cleanest non-Iran-conditional structural LNG long, but the equity has given back the crisis premium it carried Feb-Apr.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-27 C6 (Cheniere -10.3% MoM — LNG-equity crisis trade unwinding despite tight physical; paper-physical bifurcation)
Intel dossier 2026-05-27 A1 (US strikes), A2 (Iran non-retaliation), A3 (Lebanon ground op / US warns vs Beirut)
Markets dossier 2026-05-27 C4 (US LNG terminals near-max utilization; ~1/5 global LNG offline)
recent_signals.verification: Cheniere $232.03 / -10.3% MoM = CONFIRMED operational tape (yfinance); LNG-equity crisis-trade unwind = CONFIRMED (part of the broader paper crisis-trade unwind, markets C6); physical LNG terminals near-max / ~1/5 global LNG offline = CONFIRMED (markets C4); Qatar Ras Laffan FM 3-5yr structural = CONFIRMED carried-forward; reversibility split = STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS.

recent_signals.headline: **CHENIERE NODE CREATED — +2.4% TO $247.74 ON RAISED 2026 EBITDA GUIDE TO $7.25-7.75B + EU STORAGE 141 CARGOES BELOW 5-YEAR LOW.** Largest US LNG exporter; transmission mechanism between European TTF basis ↔ Hormuz LNG dislocation ↔ US LNG export economics. Cheniere management commentary on European storage 141 cargoes below 5-year low gives structural visibility into European emergency-supply needs. Wall Street upgrades post-EBITDA-guide raise. **Tuesday's +2.4% on a Brent -1.6% / WTI -4.7% day is the cleanest demonstration that war premium has migrated out of crude flat-price into LNG basis** — Cheniere is decoupled from spot oil because cash flows are multi-year contracted offtake, not commodity-linked. Inside the structural-bid trio (XLE, Frontline, Cheniere), Cheniere shares the Hormuz-still-closed structural premium with the other two — all three moved UP on Brent-down day. **The Hormuz reopening (Baker Hughes call: not until 2H26) is the only structural reverse** for this trade.
sources: Cheniere management commentary (European storage 141 cargoes below 5y low)
recent_signals.verification: Cheniere +2.4% = CONFIRMED operational tape; $7.25-7.75B EBITDA guide = CONFIRMED (Cheniere primary + Oilprice + 24/7 Wall St); EU storage 141 cargoes below 5y low = REPORTED (Cheniere management commentary); structural-bid trio inverse-to-Brent move = CONFIRMED operational pattern.
